layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE LAYOUT. The slide uses a single-container structure with a compact title area at the top (10–15% of slide height) followed by a dominant table container filling the remaining 85–90% of vertical space. The table is a complex multi-column grid with 5 columns (Workstream, Details, Owner(s), and 3 timeline columns for 2026 Q2, Q3/Q4, and 2027) and 5 major workstream rows. The layout is designed to maximize table legibility and data density while maintaining clear visual hierarchy through structured row separators, column headers, and content-aware spacing. No outer content container is used — the table sits directly on the slide background with appropriate margins. The design follows Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) principles: no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey row label cells, and dashed separator lines between workstream sections.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.12, 0.88]

Containers:
  C0 (TITLE AREA): Compact title zone at the top of the slide, occupying approximately 10–12% of slide height. Contains the slide title 'Fund Accounting platform initiative workplan' in heading font, brand primary color, left-aligned. The title sits on a single line with generous left margin (aligned to the table left edge below). A subtitle or context label 'Deliverables against each workstream' appears directly below the title in body font, brand text color, slightly smaller than the title. A visible vertical gap (approximately 2–3% of slide height) separates the title area from the table container below. No background fill, no border — the title area is open space with text elements only. The yellow annotation box visible in the source image is editorial markup and must be removed entirely from the output.
  C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE): Dominant table container occupying 85–90% of slide height, spanning nearly full slide width (3% left/right margins for safe area). This is a 5-column × 5-row workplan grid built as a Marvin table using shapes and text boxes (Section 4 Option C: Flat Geometry Table). 

COLUMN STRUCTURE (left to right, proportional widths):
1. Workstream column (18% width): Row label cells with light grey fill, bold text, top-left aligned, small left inset. Contains workstream names: 'i. Conduct discovery', 'ii. Select and onboard partner', 'iii. Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC', 'iv. Implement fund accounting platform - Holdings', 'v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments'.
2. Details column (22% width): Plain text on white background, body font, brand text color, top-left aligned. Contains task descriptions for each workstream row.
3. Owner(s) column (20% width): Plain text on white background, body font, brand text color, top-left aligned. Contains owner names with role prefixes (Finance/Ops:, Tech:) in different text runs — role prefixes in regular weight, names in bold.
4. 2026 Q2 column (15% width): Deliverables for Q2 timeline, bullet points, body font, top-left aligned.
5. Q3/Q4 + 2027 columns (combined 25% width, split into two sub-columns): Deliverables for later timeline phases, bullet points, body font, top-left aligned.

HEADER ROW TREATMENT:
No filled header bar. Column headers are bold text, body font size, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset. Directly beneath each header label: a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height — approximately 3–4pt, narrow width — roughly 20–25% of column width, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned with the header text, sitting fully below the text with no overlap). Immediately below and touching the accent tab: a thin solid horizontal line (brand dark color, full column width with small white gutters between columns — not a single full-width line spanning the entire table). This creates a three-layer header treatment: text → accent tab → solid line.

ROW STRUCTURE:
Each of the 5 workstream rows is a major section containing multiple sub-rows (one per Details item, Owner assignment, or timeline deliverable). Within each workstream section, content is distributed vertically using equal flexGrow on sub-rows so that all columns align horizontally at the same row boundaries. This is critical: row 1 in the Workstream column must align exactly with row 1 in the Details column, Owner(s) column, and all timeline columns.

ROW SEPARATORS:
Thin dashed grey horizontal lines between workstream sections (between workstream i and ii, between ii and iii, etc.). Separators span the full table width. No separator after the last workstream (workstream v). Within each workstream section, sub-rows are separated by minimal whitespace (no additional separator lines within a section — only between major workstream sections).

CONTENT DENSITY AND SPACING:
This is a DENSE slide — 5 workstreams × multiple sub-rows per workstream, with substantial text content in the Details and timeline columns. Apply Beauty Principle 0.1: padding must be minimal to prevent content overflow. Reduce internal cell padding to the minimum needed to keep text from touching cell borders. Dashed separator lines (Beauty Principle 0.10) are mandatory here — they provide visual rhythm and section boundaries without consuming vertical space. Font size must remain uniform across all body text cells (Beauty Principle C6) — do not reduce font size in one column to fit content; instead reduce padding first, then reduce container height if necessary.

VISUAL HIERARCHY:
Workstream label cells (light grey fill, bold text) are the primary visual anchors for each row section. The accent tabs beneath column headers create a secondary visual anchor at the top of the table. Timeline column headers (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) use a colored background fill (light brand accent tint) to distinguish them from the Workstream/Details/Owner(s) columns, which have white header backgrounds. This creates a clear left-side (descriptive) vs. right-side (timeline) visual split.

ALIGNMENT:
All column left edges must snap to a consistent vertical grid. All row top edges within a workstream section must align horizontally across all 5 columns (Beauty Principle B2). Dashed separator lines must be at identical vertical positions across all columns.

TABLE BACKGROUND:
No outer container border or background fill. The table sits directly on the slide's white background. Individual cells have fills as described (light grey for workstream labels, white for all other cells, light brand tint for timeline column headers).
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The table container fills the slide effectively and does not require a decorative background layer for visual grouping or fill. The slide's content density is high, and adding a bottom layer would not provide visual benefit. No SC-BOTTOM element is rendered.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. This slide does not contain cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. All content is contained within the single table structure. No SC-TOP element is rendered.

_reference_violations.description: C0 (TITLE AREA) and C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE) overlap visibly. The title text 'Fund Accounting platform initiative workplan' and subtitle 'Deliverables against each workstream' are positioned directly over the table header row, with the table column headers ('Workstream', 'Details', 'Owner(s)', timeline columns) appearing beneath and overlapping with the title text. The layout description specifies a visible vertical gap (2-3% of slide height) separating the title area from the table container below, but no such gap exists in the rendered sketch.

C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE): Column/row transposition errors detected. Multiple cells contain content that does not match the source slide's column-row positions. For example, in the 2026 Q2 column, the content appears scrambled and overlapping across multiple rows, with text from different workstream sections bleeding into each other. The Q3, Q4, and 2027 columns show similar transposition issues where deliverables are not aligned to their correct workstream rows. The table structure shows 5 workstream rows as expected, but the cell content placement within timeline columns does not preserve the source data's row-by-row correspondence.

C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE): Row alignment failure across columns. The workstream rows do not maintain consistent horizontal alignment across all 5 columns. For example, the content in the 2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, and 2027 columns does not align with the corresponding workstream row boundaries established by the Workstream and Details columns. Text in timeline columns appears to float vertically without respecting the row structure defined by the left-side columns.

C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE): Text overflow and clipping visible in multiple timeline columns (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027). The content in these columns appears compressed, overlapping, and in some cases illegible, indicating that the column widths or row heights are insufficient for the volume of bullet-point deliverables specified in the source data. The layout description specifies this is a DENSE slide requiring minimal padding, but the current rendering shows content overflow rather than proper fit.
